mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in >60% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand that depresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline in retail sales and housing starts fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth coincides with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in >50% of observations during early-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates beyond a 4.0% annual threshold, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening that compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 4.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion that boosts consumer spending forecasts and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined payroll growth and unemployment decline signal fails to generate a statistically significant positive Rank IC over 60-day forward returns in a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly declines in real gross domestic product confirm an economic contraction that reduces corporate profit margins and triggers sustained negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Negative GDP growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in >55% of recessionary quarters during walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month drop in industrial production exceeding 1.0% signals a sharp manufacturing slowdown that forces earnings downgrades and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in less than 40% of periods where industrial production falls by more than 1.0%.
